title: commelina edulis
intro: Commelina edulis, also known as the Vining Spurred Coralroot or Common Dayflower, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, Asia, and the Pacific Islands. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Commelina edulis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Commelina edulis are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for their medicinal properties.
